title: Schmidth Hammer Test
aim: To Determine the rebound hardness no. of given rock samples using Schmidth Hammer.
theory:
• Schmidth Hammer is a non Destructive test for quality of rock or concrete determination.
  • It measures the rebound hardness of the tested material.
  • The mechanism is simple it has a Spring and a Plunger.
  • As the Spring is released the plunger makes impact on the specimen.
  • This impact creates a rebound and the rebound can be read directly form the numerical scale.
  • This uses the prediction of Uni-axial Compressive strength to predict.
  • There are two types of Schmidth Hammer - a) L type (0.735 Nm which is 1/3 of N type) b) OFC, N Type.
  • As per ISRM for 20-150MPa approx UCS L type must be used.
procedure: 20 Points should be marked on the smooth surface of the rock/concrete specimen to be tested.
There should be more than 2-4units distance from the edge of the rock specimen.
The distance between any two points must be more than 6mm.
The apparatus must be placed and pressed agianst the surface and the reading must be note down after locking the reading.
The Spring must be release and the same should be repeated for other 19 points and the upper 10 points average must be calculated.
Similarly, this should be repeated for other 9 samples.
calculation: Average of the top 10 values of each samples observation
precautions:
• The apparatus must be perpendicular to the sample.
  • The apparatus and the specimen must be in proper contact.
result:
Rebound number of the given specimen is as below:
  • Sample A - 51.8
  • Sample B - 52.9
  • Sample C - 51.3
  • Sample D - 19.9
  • Sample E - 23.2
  • Sample F - 24.5
  • Sample G - 30.4
  • Sample H - 19.8
  • Sample I - 29.3
  • Sample J - 63.2
